title: DDI-CDI Data Structure
description: Data organization based on reusable data structure components.

anyOf.description: Data organization based on reusable data structure components.
Role of a set of data structure components for content referencing purposes

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

anyOf.description: Role of a data structure component for content referencing purposes

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

anyOf.description: Role of a data structure component for content identification purposes

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

anyOf.description: Structure of a dimensional data set (organized collection of multidimensional data). It is described by dimension, measure and attribute components.
Role given to a represented variable in the context of a data structure to hold the observed/derived values.

cdi:name.description: Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:semantic.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.

anyOf.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a dimensional data structure to identify the universes associated with data points. The variable acts as a field in the compound identifier (the key structure) to disambiguate the cells in the multi-dimensional "cube".  AG: references to the variables which are the components of a compound identifier, in which each variable is a single field - an axis - in a coordinate system addressing a location in a matrix. These variables are often categorical, but also commonly include time. Unlike other Components, they may be ordered using instances of cdi:ComponentPosition.
Structure of a long dataset (organized collection of long data). It is described by identifier, measure, attribute, variable descriptor and variable value components.

cdi:has_DataStructureComponent.description: A LongDataStructure must include exactly one IdentifierComponent, exactly one VariableDescriptorComponent, exactly one VariableValueComponent, plus zero or more AttributeComponents.

anyOf.description: Structure of a wide dataset (organized collection of wide data). It is described by identifier, measure and attribute components.

CdifDataStructureComponent.title: DDI-CDI Data Structure Component
CdifDataStructureComponent.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a long or wide data structure to identify the units associated to data points, and in dimensional and key value data structures to provide identifying fields for the instance values.

anyOf.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a data structure to hold the observed/derived values.

cdi:name.description: Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:semantic.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.

anyOf.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a dimensional data structure to identify the universes associated with data points. The variable acts as a field in the compound identifier (the key structure) to disambiguate the cells in the multi-dimensional "cube".  AG: references to the variables which are the components of a compound identifier, in which each variable is a single field - an axis - in a coordinate system addressing a location in a matrix. These variables are often categorical, but also commonly include time. Unlike other Components, they may be ordered using instances of cdi:ComponentPosition.

CdifRepresentedVariable.title: DDI-CDI Represented Variable
CdifRepresentedVariable.description: Conceptual variable with a substantive value domain specified.

cdi:describedUnitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the data values are measured (kg, pound, euro), expressed as a value from a controlled system of entries (i.e., QDT). Supports the provision of an identifier for the entry in the authoritative source (a URI, etc.), and the specific vocabulary.

cdi:hasIntendedDataType.description: The data type intended to be used by this variable. Supports the optional use of an external controlled vocabulary.

anyOf.description: Value domain for a sentinel conceptual domain.

anyOf.title: CDIF Enumeration Domain
anyOf.description: A base class acting as an extension point to allow a codification vocabulary to be documented as enumerated value domain.

schema:name.description: Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic).

cdif:references.description: SKOS concept scheme that contains concepts defining the allowed values of this enumeration domain.

anyOf.title: SKOS Concept Scheme
anyOf.description: JSON Schema for a SKOS ConceptScheme in JSON-LD form. Validates the root object as a skos:ConceptScheme with prefLabel, hasTopConcept, and nested skos:Concept items. References the skosConcept and skosCollection building blocks. Based on the W3C SKOS Reference (https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/) and the SKOS RDF vocabulary (https://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core.rdf).

@context.description: JSON-LD context declaring the skos namespace prefix and any additional prefixes used in concept URIs. May be a single object or an array of strings and objects.

@id.description: URI identifier for this concept scheme.

skos:prefLabel.description: Preferred lexical label for the concept scheme. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

skos:altLabel.description: Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, spelling variants).

skos:hiddenLabel.description: Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.

skos:definition.description: Formal explanation of the meaning or purpose of this concept scheme.

skos:notation.description: Classification code or notation for this concept scheme.

skos:note.description: General note about the concept scheme.

skos:scopeNote.description: Note clarifying the intended scope of the concept scheme.

skos:historyNote.description: Note about the history of the concept scheme.

skos:changeNote.description: Note documenting a change to the concept scheme.

skos:editorialNote.description: Note for editors, translators, and maintainers.

skos:example.description: Example of the use of this concept scheme.

skos:hasTopConcept.description: Top-level concepts in this scheme. Each item is a skos:Concept (inline or @id reference).

@id.description: URI reference to a concept defined elsewhere

dcterms:creator.description: Agent(s) who created this concept scheme.

dcterms:created.description: Date the concept scheme was created (ISO 8601).

dcterms:modified.description: Date the concept scheme was last modified (ISO 8601).

dcterms:title.description: Title of the concept scheme (Dublin Core). Prefer skos:prefLabel for the SKOS label; dcterms:title may be used for additional metadata.

dcterms:description.description: Description of the concept scheme (Dublin Core).

dcterms:rights.description: Rights statement for the concept scheme.

schema:version.description: Version identifier for the concept scheme.

anyOf.description: object reference via URI or URI fragment referencing an @id in the same document

cdif:purpose.description: Intent or reason for the object/the description of the object.

anyOf.description: JSON-LD @id reference to a node defined elsewhere in the graph

@id.description: IRI or blank node identifier of the referenced node

cdif:displayLabel.description: A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

cdif:recommendedDataType.description: The data types that are recommended for use with this domain.

cdi:takesSentinelValuesFrom.description: Sentinel (missing / not-applicable) value domain(s) for this RepresentedVariable (RepresentedVariable.takesSentinelValuesFrom).

anyOf.description: Value domain for a substantive conceptual domain. Typically a description and/or enumeration of allowed values of interest.

anyOf.title: CDIF Enumeration Domain
anyOf.description: A base class acting as an extension point to allow a codification vocabulary to be documented as enumerated value domain.

schema:name.description: Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic).

cdif:references.description: SKOS concept scheme that contains concepts defining the allowed values of this enumeration domain.

References the skosConcept and skosCollection building blocks. Based on the W3C SKOS Reference (https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/) and the SKOS RDF vocabulary (https://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core.rdf).

@id.description: URI identifier for this concept scheme.

skos:prefLabel.description: Preferred lexical label for the concept scheme. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

skos:altLabel.description: Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, spelling variants).

skos:hiddenLabel.description: Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.

skos:notation.description: Classification code or notation for this concept scheme.

skos:example.description: Example of the use of this concept scheme.

skos:hasTopConcept.description: Top-level concepts in this scheme. Each item is a skos:Concept (inline or @id reference).

@id.description: URI reference to a concept defined elsewhere

dcterms:creator.description: Agent(s) who created this concept scheme.

dcterms:created.description: Date the concept scheme was created (ISO 8601).

dcterms:modified.description: Date the concept scheme was last modified (ISO 8601).

dcterms:title.description: Title of the concept scheme (Dublin Core). Prefer skos:prefLabel for the SKOS label; dcterms:title may be used for additional metadata.

dcterms:description.description: Description of the concept scheme (Dublin Core).

dcterms:rights.description: Rights statement for the concept scheme.

cdif:purpose.description: Intent or reason for the object/the description of the object.

anyOf.description: JSON-LD @id reference to a node defined elsewhere in the graph

@id.description: IRI or blank node identifier of the referenced node

cdif:displayLabel.description: A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

cdif:recommendedDataType.description: The data types that are recommended for use with this domain.

cdi:takesSubstantiveValuesFrom.description: The substantive value domain for this RepresentedVariable - the set of valid, meaningful values (RepresentedVariable.takesSubstantiveValuesFrom).

cdi:simpleUnitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the data values are measured (kg, pound, euro), expressed as a simple string, in cases where no additional information is available (in the legacy system) or needed (as in the case of broad agreement within the community of use [i.e., ISO country codes, currencies, etc. in SDMX])

cdi:descriptiveText.description: A short natural language account of the characteristics of the object.

anyOf.description: Conceptual domain of sentinel concepts.

cdi:displayLabel.description: A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

anyOf.description: Formal description of a set of values.

cdi:classificationLevel.description: Indicates the type of relationship, nominal, ordinal, interval, ratio, or continuous. Use where appropriate for the representation type.

cdi:description.description: A formal description of the set of values in human-readable language.

cdi:formatPattern.description: A pattern for a number as described in Unicode Locale Data Markup Language (LDML) (http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/tr35.html) Part 3: Numbers (http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/tr35-numbers.html#Number_Format_Patterns) and Part 4. Dates (http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/tr35-dates.html#Date_Format_Patterns) . Examples would be #,##0.### to describe the pattern for a decimal number, or yyyy.MM.dd G 'at' HH:mm:ss zzz for a datetime pattern.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:logicalExpression.description: A logical expression where the values of "x" making the expression true are the members of the set of valid values. For example, "(all reals x such that x > 0)" describes the real numbers greater than 0.

cdi:maximumValueExclusive.description: A string denoting the maximum possible value (excluding this value). From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "maxExclusive: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the maximum valid value (exclusive). The value of this property becomes the maximum exclusive annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

cdi:maximumValueInclusive.description: A string denoting the maximum possible value. From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "maximum: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the maximum valid value (inclusive); equivalent to maxInclusive. The value of this property becomes the maximum annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

cdi:minimumValueExclusive.description: A string denoting the minimum possible value (excluding this value). From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "minExclusive: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the minimum valid value (exclusive). The value of this property becomes the minimum exclusive annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

cdi:minimumValueInclusive.description: A string denoting the minimum possible value. From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "minimum: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the minimum valid value (inclusive); equivalent to minInclusive. The value of this property becomes the minimum annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

cdi:regularExpression.description: A regular expression where strings matching the expression belong to the set of valid values. Use typeOfContent to specify the syntax of the regularExpression found in content.

cdif:content.description: Content of the property expressed as a simple string.

cdi:typeOfContent.description: Optional use of a controlled vocabulary to specifically type the associated content.

anyOf.description: Set of concepts structured by the relations among them [GSIM 1.1].

cdi:allowsDuplicates.description: If value is False, the members are unique within the collection - if True, there may be duplicates. (Note that a mathematical “bag” permits duplicates and is unordered - a “set” does not have duplicates and may be ordered.)

cdi:externalDefinition.description: A reference to an external definition of a concept (that is, a concept which is described outside the content of the DDI-CDI metadata description). An example is a SKOS concept. The definition property is assumed to duplicate the external one referenced if externalDefinition is used. Other corresponding properties are assumed to be included unchanged if used.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:name.description: Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

cdi:purpose.description: Intent or reason for the object/the description of the object.

anyOf.description: Unit type is a type or class of objects of interest (units).

cdi:descriptiveText.description: A short natural language account of the characteristics of the object.

cdi:definition.description: Natural language statement conveying the meaning of a concept, differentiating it from other concepts. Supports the use of multiple languages and structured text. 'externalDefinition' can't be used if 'definition' is used.

cdi:displayLabel.description: A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

cdi:externalDefinition.description: A reference to an external definition of a concept (that is, a concept which is described outside the content of the DDI-CDI metadata description). An example is a SKOS concept. The definition property is assumed to duplicate the external one referenced if externalDefinition is used. Other corresponding properties are assumed to be included unchanged if used.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:name.description: Human understandable name (linguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

cdi:unitOfMeasureKind.description: Kind of unit of measure, so that it may be prone to translation to equivalent UOMs. Example values include "acceleration," "temperature," "salinity", etc. This description exists at the conceptual level, indicating a limitation on the type of representations which may be used for the variable as it is made more concrete.

cdi:definition.description: Natural language statement conveying the meaning of a concept, differentiating it from other concepts. Supports the use of multiple languages and structured text. 'externalDefinition' can't be used if 'definition' is used.

cdi:displayLabel.description: A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

cdi:externalDefinition.description: A reference to an external definition of a concept (that is, a concept which is described outside the content of the DDI-CDI metadata description). An example is a SKOS concept. The definition property is assumed to duplicate the external one referenced if externalDefinition is used. Other corresponding properties are assumed to be included unchanged if used.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:name.description: Human understandable name (linguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

SkosConcept.title: SKOS Concept
SkosConcept.description: JSON Schema for a SKOS Concept in JSON-LD form. A unit of thought within a concept scheme. Supports labels (prefLabel, altLabel, hiddenLabel), notations, hierarchical relations (broader/narrower), associative relations (related), cross-scheme mapping properties, scheme membership, and documentary notes. Based on the W3C SKOS Reference (https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/).

@context.description: JSON-LD context declaring the skos namespace prefix. May be a single object or an array of strings and objects.

skos:prefLabel.description: Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

skos:hiddenLabel.description: Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.

skos:notation.description: Classification code for this concept within a scheme.

skos:definition.description: Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.

skos:scopeNote.description: Note clarifying meaning and/or intended use.

skos:historyNote.description: Note about past state or use.

skos:example.description: Example of use of this concept.

skos:inScheme.description: Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.

skos:topConceptOf.description: Concept scheme(s) for which this is a top concept.

skos:broader.description: Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.

skos:narrower.description: Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.

skos:exactMatch.description: Concepts in other schemes with equivalent meaning.

skos:closeMatch.description: Concepts in other schemes with similar meaning.

Identifier.description:
Properties for a schema.org identifier (schema:PropertyValue pattern).

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as
schema:Identifier / schema:PropertyValue is represented by a single attribute
of that class type. The JSON Schema implementation permits the property value
to be EITHER a plain string (interpreted as the bare identifier value) OR a
full schema:PropertyValue object (with explicit @type, propertyID, value).
Consumers should accept either form.

schema:propertyID.description: In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability

schema:url.description: web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ

@type.description: Must include both schema:PropertyValue and cdi:InstanceVariable. Additional types may be included.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type

cdif:physicalDataType.description: identifier or name for the data type concept.

cdif:role.description: Specifies the role this variable plays in a data structure. UnitIdentifier names the unit a row describes; Measure holds observed/derived values; Attribute qualifies an observation; Dimension addresses a cell in a multi-dimensional cube; Descriptor names the variable that a Reference column records values for (long format); ReferenceVariable holds those recorded values.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type
reference to a skos concept for the property

cdif:uses.description: Essentially the same as schema:propertyID. References to concepts that this variable measures or represents. When the dataset's distribution carries cdi:isStructuredBy (CDIF Data Structure profile), cdif:uses references the RepresentedVariable that supplies the represented-variable-level properties below, which are then NOT duplicated on the InstanceVariable.

cdi:function.description: Immutable characteristic of the variable such as geographic designator, weight, temporal designation, etc. (InstanceVariable.function).

cdi:platformType.description: The application or technical system context in which the variable has been realized - typically a statistical processing package or processing environment (InstanceVariable.platformType).

cdi:source.description: Reference capturing provenance information for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.source).

cdif:isDescribedBy_StatisticsCollection.description: The StatisticsCollection holding summary / category statistics for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.isDescribedBy). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI isDescribedBy association is polymorphic.

cdi:hasIntendedDataType.description: The data type intended to be used by this variable, independent of its physical representation (RepresentedVariable.hasIntendedDataType).

cdi:describedUnitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the data values are measured, expressed as a controlled-vocabulary entry (RepresentedVariable.describedUnitOfMeasure). For a plain-string unit, use cdif:simpleUnitOfMeasure instead.

cdi:qualifies.description: reference to an instance variable defined for this dataset

allOf.description: Schema.org PropertyValue-based variableMeasured. Defines properties for a measured variable in a dataset, typed as schema:PropertyValue.

schema:name.description: string label associated with the variable in the dataset serialization

items.description: human intelligible name for variable that conveys semantics

schema:measurementTechnique.description: Text description or URI specifying how values for the variable were obtained.

schema:propertyID.description: identifier or name for the property concept

schema:unitCode.description: URI or code identifying the unit of measure

schema:minValue.description: minimum numeric value in the dataset

schema:maxValue.description: maximum numeric value in the dataset

anyOf.description: link to a web page useful for interpreting the variable
Building block to implement relations to other resources using a URL and some semantics.

allOf.description: schema for a labeled link, a profile of schema.org/CreativeWork
DCAT Relationship surface added on top of the schema.org labeled link construct.

dcat:hadRole.description: Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).

dcterms:relation.description: URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).

DefinedTerm.description:
schema.org Defined Term schema.

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as
schema:DefinedTerm is represented by a single attribute of that class type.
The JSON Schema implementation permits the property value to be EITHER a
plain string (interpreted as schema:name or schema:termCode in the default
vocabulary) OR a full schema:DefinedTerm object (with explicit @type, name,
identifier, termCode, inDefinedTermSet). Consumers should accept either form.

Note that this differs from the cdif Concept policy: a Concept (skos:Concept)
in CDIF profiles MUST be a controlled-vocabulary entry (object form or
@id-reference) — plain strings are not permitted for Concept-typed values
because vocabulary identity cannot be recovered from an unscoped label.

schema:name.description: text label for the term that is useful to human user

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.

schema:termCode.description: A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation

IdentifierComponent.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a long or wide data structure to identify the units associated to data points, and in dimensional and key value data structures to provide identifying fields for the instance values.

AttributeComponent.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a data structure to qualify observations or provide other types of supplementary information.

anyOf.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a data structure.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:semantic.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:semantic.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.

VariableValueComponent.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a data structure to record values of multiple variables.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:semantic.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.

VariableDescriptorComponent.description: Role given to a represented variable in the context of a data structure to provide codes for variable identification.

cdif:isDefinedBy_DescriptorVariable.title: DDI-CDI Descriptor Variable
cdif:isDefinedBy_DescriptorVariable.description: Variable that provides codes for variable identification in the context of a data structure. Descriptor Variables hold values which reference the logical variables in the data set, indicating which one the associated value in the corresponding Reference Variable is a measure/value for. Descriptor Variables are presentational variables found only in Long Data Sets.

cdif:hasValuesFrom.description: A cdi:DescriptorValueDomain enumerating the codes that can appear in the descriptor column, each paired (via cdif:isDefinedBy) with the RepresentedVariable that the code names.

cdif:takesValuesFrom.description: Ordered list of descriptor entries. Each entry has a cdif:value (the code as it appears in the descriptor column) and a cdif:isDefinedBy pointer to the RepresentedVariable that the code names.

cdif:value.description: The code value as it appears in the descriptor column.

anyOf.description: object reference via URI or URI fragment to a RepresentedVariable defined elsewhere in the document

cdi:name.description: Human understandable name (linguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:semantic.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.

id-reference.description: JSON-LD @id reference to a node defined elsewhere in the graph

@id.description: IRI or blank node identifier of the referenced node

cdi:languageSpecificString.description: The label text with language tag

anyOf.description: Non-DDI identifier (dt-NonDdiIdentifier)

cdi:context.description: Context or usage of this name

LanguageTaggedValue.description: An RDF literal value with a language tag, serialized as a JSON-LD value object.

@language.description: BCP 47 language tag (e.g. en, fr, de).

ConceptRef.description: A reference to a SKOS Concept by URI.

StatisticsCollection.title: StatisticsCollection
StatisticsCollection.description: Groups one or more Statistics nodes for an instance variable. A typical use is a dataset-level collection holding row-count / mean / stddev Statistics for each measured variable.

cdi:hasWeight.description: The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

cdif:has_Statistics.description: Statistics nodes carried by this collection (inline or @id-ref). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.

cdif:indexedBy.description: CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) the contained Statistics index — the collection-level coordinate space.

Statistics.title: Statistics
Statistics.description: A named bundle of one or more Statistic value objects for an instance variable, optionally weighted, optionally broken down by Category.

cdi:typeOfStatistic.title: SKOS Concept (CDIF vocabulary-bound term reference)
cdi:typeOfStatistic.description: Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic — e.g. mean, median, count, sum, stdDev.

cdi:statistic.description: Ordered list of Statistic value objects carried by this bundle. Order is significant — consumers MAY rely on array position.

items.title: Statistic
items.description: DDI-CDI Statistic dataType — a single computed value (mean, count, median, etc.). A value object with no identity; appears inline inside a cdi:statistic array.

cdi:computationBase.description: The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).

cdi:content.description: The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.

cdi:isWeighted.description: True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.

cdi:typeOfNumericValue.description: The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.

cdi:hasWeight.description: The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights when computing the Statistic entries (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

cdif:appliesTo.description: CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) this Statistics bundle summarizes — the per-bundle "what these numbers describe" link. When a Statistics node sits inside a StatisticsCollection that indexes more than one variable, cdif:appliesTo disambiguates which variable each bundle describes.

cdif:has_CategoryStatistics.description: CategoryStatistics entries breaking this Statistics bundle down by Category. cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.

CategoryStatistics.title: CategoryStatistics
CategoryStatistics.description: Statistics for a specific Category of an instance variable within a data set.

cdi:for.description: The Category this CategoryStatistics is for (inline Category node or an @id-reference).

anyOf.title: Category
anyOf.description: Minimal Category shape used as a CategoryStatistics target. CDIF simplifies the DDI-CDI InternationalString / LabelForDisplay / ObjectName valued properties to plain strings carried under the cdif: namespace. A full CDIF treatment of categories lives in cdifEnumerationDomain / SKOS Concept.

cdif:name.description: Human-understandable name(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.name (ObjectName) to plain strings.

cdif:descriptiveText.description: Short natural-language account of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.descriptiveText (InternationalString).

cdif:definition.description: Natural-language definition of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.definition (InternationalString).

cdif:displayLabel.description: Human-readable display label(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.displayLabel (LabelForDisplay).

cdi:typeOfStatistic.title: SKOS Concept (CDIF vocabulary-bound term reference)
cdi:typeOfStatistic.description: Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic.

items.title: Statistic
items.description: DDI-CDI Statistic dataType — a single computed value (mean, count, median, etc.). A value object with no identity; appears inline inside a cdi:statistic array.

cdi:computationBase.description: The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).

cdi:content.description: The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.

cdi:isWeighted.description: True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.

cdi:typeOfNumericValue.description: The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.

cdi:hasWeight.description: The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

PrimaryKey.description: Role of an ordered set of cdi:InstanceVariables that uniquely identify a data instance. Array order of cdif:isComposedOf items is the position; no intermediate ComponentPosition wrapper.

cdif:isComposedOf.description: Ordered list of cdi:InstanceVariables (inline cdifInstanceVariable or @id-reference). Position is implicit in array order.

cdi:language.description: ISO language code (e.g. en, fr, de)

ControlledVocabularyEntry.description: DDI-CDI controlled vocabulary entry (dt-ControlledVocabularyEntry). Allows for unstructured content which may be an entry from an externally maintained controlled vocabulary. When the content is from a controlled vocabulary, provide the code value of the entry along with a reference to the vocabulary from which the value is taken.

cdi:entryReference.description: A reference to the specific item in the vocabulary referenced by cdi:vocabulary, using a URI or other resolvable identifier.

cdi:entryValue.description: The value of the entry of the controlled vocabulary. If no controlled vocabulary is used the term is entered here and none of the properties defining the controlled vocabulary location are used.

cdi:name.description: The name of the code list (controlled vocabulary).

cdi:valueForOther.description: If the value of the string is "Other" or the equivalent from the codelist, this attribute can provide a more specific value not found in the codelist.

cdi:vocabulary.description: A reference to the external controlled vocabulary, using a URI or other resolvable identifier.
